summaries.64143: Comments confirm the listener is still being called extra times through at least 3.27.4, with no fix in sight. One commenter notes the double-fire on focus gain is expected behavior; the multi-call on `controller.clear()` inside the listener is the true regression, reproducible on both stable and master. No PR or workaround identified in comments; issue remains stalled.
summaries.163607: Reproduced on stable 3.29 and master 3.30 on mobile Chrome (Android); one commenter linked this to similar issues #124483, #162231, and #162698. A Flutter engineer confirmed the bug occurs on Android mobile Chrome but not on desktop Chrome. One commenter narrowed the cause to scroll position interaction with the number of text lines relative to the viewport. No fix or PR referenced; stalled.
summaries.120551: Comments confirm this is Android-specific; a triager compared with Telegram (handles above keyboard) vs. iOS apps (handles can go below keyboard). A Flutter engineer and an Android engineer both concluded drawing over the keyboard is not straightforwardly possible from the Flutter view layer, and that native Android also allows handles to hang off screen. No fix planned; the suggested workaround is to keep the text field away from the screen edge.
summaries.144162: Root cause identified: `Form.save()` iterates a `Set` of fields in non-deterministic order, so the outer `FormField`'s `onSaved` may run before the inner `TextFormField`'s `onSaved` calls `state.didChange()`, leaving the outer field stale. A commenter suggested using `Form.onChanged` or doing all saving in the parent's `onSaved` as workarounds. No PR filed; issue remains open awaiting team input on correct behavior.
summaries.40477: Reproduced on stable 2.5 and master 2.6. PR #123907 was linked as fixing `prefixIcon` vertical alignment but a commenter noted `prefix` (inline widget) aligns differently and needs further design consideration before being fully addressed. No fix specifically for `prefix` with `isDense`; stalled pending design decision.
summaries.120852: Confirmed macOS-only; not reproduced on web or mobile. In 3.7.3, the regression worsened — the left arrow key now also deletes text preceding the composing range, not just text inside it. A separate sub-issue about the macOS error sound on arrow key press was requested to be filed separately. No PR or fix referenced; stalled.
summaries.111054: Comments confirm the context menu stays visible through 3.24 on macOS when the app loses focus by clicking the top menu bar (app lifecycle does not change to inactive in that case). A contributor investigated and found the issue is not a focus or lifecycle problem but that specific macOS events (clicking menu bar, dragging window) do not trigger context menu dismissal. PRs welcome per a maintainer comment but none submitted; stalled.
summaries.156659: Confirmed Android-only (iOS unfocuses the TextField correctly when tapping the WebView, which is considered correct behavior). One commenter noted the framework does not send `TextInput.hide`, pointing to the engine as the source; related to #130652. Another commenter reported the same issue on Firefox web with a `PointerInterceptor`-wrapped WebView. No fix or PR; stalled.
summaries.88299: Root cause identified: the issue is keyboard-specific — it only reproduces with AOSP keyboard (emulator without Google Play) and SwiftKey, not Gboard. The framework correctly updates `_currentConfiguration` with the new action; the keyboard on screen simply does not update. A commenter confirmed native Android apps update correctly with the same keyboard, suggesting the problem is in how Flutter signals the IME about configuration changes. No fix or PR; stalled.
summaries.134159: Comments confirm the unexpected left-side padding (half of `letterSpacing`) on the first character also occurs in `RichText`/`Text` widgets — it is standard behavior where `letterSpacing` is split half-before and half-after each glyph. One commenter noted that with SkParagraph the behavior is different (no leading space). No fix or PR; triaged to design/engine team.
summaries.82124: Root cause identified in comments: arrow keys and other editing operations for a bare `TextInputClient` on macOS are now handled at the `EditableText`/`Shortcuts` layer, not the engine, so a raw client without `EditableText` does not receive them. A maintainer noted the keys should be ported to `Shortcuts` soon. No confirmed fix or PR; issue stalled with suggestion to test on other desktop platforms.
summaries.148117: Root cause traced to Android's keyboard-switching dialog sending a `hideSoftInputFromWindow` signal, but commenting out those engine calls did not fix it, suggesting the cause is subtler platform behavior. Reproducible on Android 31–34 but not Android 30. Two commenters reported it stopped occurring on their Pixel 7 after upgrading to Android 15, but confirmed it still reproduces on Android 14 when using the 'Change keyboard' dialog (not the globe key). Stalled; no fix or PR.
summaries.113005: Root cause identified in the issue body: `Focus.didUpdateWidget` unconditionally copies `skipTraversal` and `canRequestFocus` from the widget to the node even when they haven't changed, corrupting state set by `descendantsAreTraversable`. The reporter proposed a guarded copy fix and offered to send a PR. A maintainer confirmed the proposed fix direction and invited a PR with tests. No PR confirmed merged; status unclear.
summaries.27200: Comments confirm the bug reproduces through 3.6: `TextField`s in a pushed route without a `Scaffold` do not auto-scroll when the keyboard appears. The underlying mechanism (`Scaffold.resizeToAvoidBottomInset`) is what drives scroll-into-focus behavior, so screens without their own `Scaffold` don't benefit from it. No fix or PR; stalled.
summaries.115572: A commenter clarified this is distinct from the general cursor-override issue (#31952) and provided additional reproduction: the incorrect text-cursor is shown over stacked widgets only when the TextField is focused, not when unfocused. Reproducible in both CanvasKit and HTML renderers on web. Confirmed still present in 3.24.3 (October 2024). No fix or PR; stalled.
summaries.129875: Root cause identified: on iOS, the text input plugin receives `setSelectedTextRange` before `deleteBackward`, which sends an `updateEditingState` to the framework with a selection range pointing at the character to be deleted — this state becomes `oldValue` in `formatEditUpdate`, so `oldValue.selection.baseOffset` reflects the post-deletion offset rather than the pre-deletion cursor position. Confirmed as iOS-only; Android behaves correctly. No fix or PR; stalled.
summaries.137030: Comments confirm the issue is present on both stable and master. The root cause is that a `WidgetSpan`'s child `Text` widget is rendered on top of the parent `TextSpan`'s background paint layer, causing translucent background and decoration colors to visually compound. One commenter noted the behavior is expected given the rendering order but acknowledged it is confusing. No fix or PR; stalled pending design team input.
summaries.116420: Comments confirm the log messages (`-[UIScene(EventDeferring) _eventDeferringManager]: No viable event deferring manager found for scene`) are benign iOS system messages emitted during app termination when the keyboard is open. A maintainer noted filtering them in the Flutter tool would be difficult and a moving target. No fix planned; issue is about noise reduction rather than a functional bug.
summaries.110425: A contributor (lastmeta) worked on PR #130039 to add a `floatingLabelGap` parameter to `InputDecoration` but was blocked by repeated pushback; the contributor stated they abandoned the effort. Community members shared workarounds: a custom `InputBorder` subclass that sets `isOutline = false` (avoiding the label-on-border gap) and a `Stack`-based approach using a borderless `TextField` with an external `Container`. PR #130039 appears stalled/abandoned.
summaries.176872: Issue is iOS-specific; only reproducible with Simplified Chinese system language and `zh_CN` in `CFBundleLocalizations`. A SwiftUI reproduction did not trigger the duplication, suggesting a Flutter-side root cause. Comments identify the failure as occurring only via the 'Change keyboard' dialog SMS autofill flow on specific devices (iPhone XR, iPhone 12 Pro Max with iOS 18.6.2) but not on iPhone 16 Pro with iOS 26. No fix or PR; stalled pending further investigation.
summaries.105514: Root cause identified: `RawKeyboard` (legacy system) maps Korean characters to different logical keys than their physical key equivalents, so `isKeyPressed(LogicalKeyboardKey.keyE)` returns `false` for Korean input. A maintainer stated no breaking changes will be made to `RawKeyboard` and recommended migrating to `HardwareKeyboard`/`KeyboardListener` and using `Shortcuts`/`SingleActivator`. A follow-up test showed that even with `Shortcuts` and `SingleActivator`, the Korean key `ㄷ` (physical Key E) does not trigger the shortcut; the maintainer acknowledged this as a separate problem to investigate.
summaries.96147: Comments confirm the bug: a `WidgetSpan` as the sole first child of a `TextField`'s `TextSpan` renders outside the TextField bounds; adding any preceding `TextSpan` corrects the layout. Reproduced on macOS stable 2.8.1 and master 2.9. No root cause analysis, fix, or PR in comments; stalled.
